feat: v2.0.0 — 23 tools with hybrid DirectApiClient backend

Add 13 new diagnostic tools via DirectApiClient for raw SYNO.* API calls
not covered by py-synologydsm-api. Hybrid architecture: existing 10 health
tools use py-synologydsm-api, new tools use lightweight HTTP client with
30s cache, session refresh, and API discovery.

New tools: discover_apis, get_disk_details, get_ssd_cache,
get_storage_pool_members, get_nfs_exports, get_services_status,
get_ups_status, get_hardware_info, get_recent_logs, get_notifications,
get_active_connections, get_users, get_update_status

Enhanced health_summary with SSD cache degradation, UPS battery, and
NFS service alerts. Enabled stateless HTTP mode for FastMCP 3.x.

- Total Net Worth now includes tokens + LP + staking positions

- LP and staking fetched in parallel for performance

docs(skill): add daily soul question example (Issue #719) (#844)

Add a comprehensive example for the 0.4.2 MVP use case: daily analysis
of chat/work records and generate open-ended "soul questions" to
trigger discussions in topic groups.

Key features:
- BBS-style mode: No expected user response
- Open discussion: Provokes thinking, not decisions
- Active push: Send to topic groups

The example provides instructions for the ScheduleAgent to:
1. Read workspace/groups.json to get topic groups
2. Read chat logs from workspace/logs/chat-messages/
3. Analyze and generate soul questions
4. Send to topic groups using send_user_feedback

Note: Users need to:
1. Mark a group as topic group using /topic-group mark <chatId>
2. Replace chatId with their topic group's chatId

Co-authored-by: hs3180 <claude@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(functions): implement request_id correlation across all handlers

- Add getRequestContext helper to use middleware-provided context
- Update API handlers (events.ts, rum.ts) to use consistent request_id
- Add Sentry scope setting in middleware (future-proof)
- Ensure request_id is attached to all logs, responses, and will be
  stored in D1 records when Sentry is configured
- Add comprehensive tests for telemetry (19 tests) and types (5 tests)

This enables end-to-end request tracing:
- Middleware generates ULID request_id
- All handlers use same request_id via context.data
- X-Request-ID header returned in all responses
- Structured logs include request_id and duration

Closes bd-cpx

security: sandbox script endpoints — clear inherited env vars

Scripts inherited the full node process environment, exposing

and FACILITATOR_SHARED_SECRET to any script endpoint.

and the 5 request-specific vars (REQUEST_METHOD, QUERY_STRING,
REQUEST_BODY, REQUEST_HEADERS, ENDPOINT_SLUG).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: suppress false NFS alert for non-admin users

The SYNO.Core.FileServ.NFS API returns nfs_enable=false for non-admin
users even when NFS is actually enabled. Only trigger the alert when the
response contains multiple fields (indicating real data, not a
permissions-limited stub response).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix air-wrap-func-with-parallel for scalar SSA chains between linalg ops (#1367) (#1368)

Replace clone+erase with moveOpBefore in WrapFuncWithParallelPattern to
preserve SSA use-def chains. The clone+erase approach fails when scalar
ops (tensor.extract, arith.divf, math.rsqrt, etc.) bridge between
structured linalg ops, because IRMapping may not fully remap transitive
dependencies. Moving ops directly avoids this issue entirely.

scored 1/10 because only the butter and syrup were detected.

fix: security hardening, data integrity, and code quality improvements

- Add IPC path validation to prevent path traversal (read-file-text, get-pdf-data, get-parsed-xml)
- Add XML escaping for all interpolated values in patient.xml and visit.xml generation
- Remove sandbox:false from main window and --no-sandbox from start script

  Patients(last_name, dob), ImportEvents(session_id, patient_id)

  main.ts debug_paths.txt)

* refactor(volunteer-signup): overhaul sign-up system with cancellation support and server-side reminders

- Delete 5 over-engineered email services (EmailConfigService, EmailLoggerService,
  EmailManagementService, ReminderService, README) and consolidate into single EmailService
- Add volunteer self-service cancellation UI (email + name lookup) on calendar page
- Add cancellation confirmation and admin notification emails
- Add server-side send-reminders Azure Function to replace broken client-side reminder system
- Add ReminderSent column to SignUps table for tracking reminded signups
- Simplify volunteer.model.ts by removing unused types (ShiftHelper, VolunteerShiftValidator, etc.)
- Simplify VolunteerShiftService by removing unused methods and reminder dependencies
- Remove EMAIL-SETUP-GUIDE.md and OAUTH-SETUP-GUIDE.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): fix Docker race condition, production data loading, and clean up stale configs

- Replace init-database.sh (hardcoded path) with docker/entrypoint.sh that runs SQL init inside the container
- Update healthcheck to verify database exists before DAB starts, eliminating the race condition
- Add data_api_location to GitHub Actions workflow to fix production "Failed to load data" errors
- Simplify npm scripts: remove db:init, use docker compose v2 syntax
- Delete stale files: .devcontainer/, .env.example, .env.oauth.example, .vscode/tasks.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ui): redesign entire site with custom design system, no Angular Material

Replace all Angular Material components with hand-written CSS using a warm,
kitchen-inspired design system. Build all 6 pages from scratch with lazy-loaded
routes, responsive layouts, scroll animations, and full accessibility.

Pages: Home (hero + stats + CTA), Volunteer (shift browsing + signup/cancel
modals), Volunteer Admin (OAuth + CRUD), Get Involved (donations + refugee
services + financial reports), Gallery (masonry + lightbox), About (mission +
FAQ + contact map).

Shared: ModalService, ToastService, Navbar, Footer, ScrollAnimateDirective.
Legacy URL redirects for all old routes. Reuses all existing backend services.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: user message triggers recovery in all unavailable states (#254)

* feat: user message triggers recovery in all unavailable states

Previously only rate_limited state responded to user messages by
attempting recovery. Now recovering and down states also accelerate
when a user message arrives:

- rate_limited: clears cooldown (existing behavior)
- recovering: resets exponential backoff to retry immediately
- down: resets downRetryInterval to probe immediately

Changes:
- heartbeat-engine.js: expand notifyUserMessage() to handle all states
- activity-monitor.js: read signal file for all non-ok states
- c4-receive.js: write signal file for all non-ok states (not just rate_limited)
- 4 new tests for recovering/down/ok/cooldown scenarios

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: reduce user message recovery cooldown from 5min to 1min

60s aligns with the typical recovery cycle duration. Users can
trigger another recovery acceleration after 1 minute if the
previous attempt hasn't restored service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct misleading "message saved" prompts when service unavailable

Messages are not actually saved to the C4 queue when health is not ok.
Update prompts to honestly tell users to resend:
- rate_limited: "Please send your message again after I'm back!"
- recovering: "Please try again in a moment!"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[#2226] Fix DER INTEGER encoding for Node 25 strict OpenSSL (#2227)

Strip leading zero bytes in derInteger() before encoding. randomBytes(16)
can produce values starting with 0x00, which is illegal padding in strict
DER encoding enforced by Node 25's OpenSSL.

Closes #2226
